title: Social capital of urban infrastructure with Bogotá's cable car
data_sources: World Bank Integrated Questionnaire for Measuring Social Capital

data_scope.setting: Bogotá, Colombia — TransMiCable cable car, Ciudad Bolívar (intervention) vs control area
data_scope.design: intervention-vs-control natural experiment (TrUST study), baseline 2018 + follow-up
data_scope.sample_size: not stated in open abstract/text; in paywalled Methods + Supplementary Tables 1-2
data_scope.data_access: restricted via SALURBAL data-use agreement; code open on GitHub

core_conclusion: TransMiCable, an aerial cable car opened in 2018 serving a low-income informal-settlement periphery of Bogotá, reshaped social capital beyond mobility. Three latent SC classes (bonding, bridging, linking) were identified, and residents in the intervention area were 3.34x more likely (95% CI 2.43-4.62) to transition from bonding to bridging social capital than a control area, indicating the transport intervention strengthened trust among friends and neighbors and underscoring community participation in urban transformation.
why_it_matters: Quantifies the social-cohesion return on transport infrastructure (trust, networks, civic engagement), not just travel-time savings, in the peripheral informal-settlement context where inequality concentrates; provides a causal-leaning natural-experiment effect size and supplies the missing social evidence for cable-car investment in the Global South.
why_nature: Couples a concrete piece of urban infrastructure to human well-being and equity; transferable lesson for peripheral-transit equity across the Global South; strong causal design on a hard-to-measure outcome; directly policy-translatable for transport investment and community participation.
added_value_to_my_research: Continuous network-accessibility (2SFCA/R2SFCA) exposure -> dose-response for the bonding-to-bridging transition
Network-segregation lens -> community detection + spatial entropy on an infrastructure shock
Mediation to mental-health endpoints -> infrastructure -> social capital -> mental health in slums
Explainable ML over multi-mode transit -> which features drive social benefit
Multi-source data fusion -> continuous mapped social-capital surface calibrated on survey
